sn3.24:1.2: As he was sitting to one side, King Pasenadi Kosala said to the Blessed One:
sn3.24:1.3: “Where, lord, should a gift be given?”
sn3.24:1.4: “Wherever the mind feels confidence, great king.”
sn3.24:1.5: “But a gift given where, lord, bears great fruit?”
sn3.24:1.6: “This (question) is one thing, great king—‘Where should a gift be given?’—while this—‘A gift given where bears great fruit?’—is something else entirely.
sn3.24:1.7: What is given to a virtuous person—rather than to an unvirtuous one—bears great fruit.
sn3.24:1.8: In that case, great king, I will ask you a counter-question. Answer as you see fit.
sn3.24:1.10: There is the case where you have a war at hand, a battle imminent.
sn3.24:1.11: A noble-warrior youth would come along—untrained, unpracticed, undisciplined, undrilled, fearful, terrified, cowardly, quick to flee.
sn3.24:1.12: Would you take him on? Would you have any use for a man like that?”
sn3.24:1.13: “No, lord, I wouldn’t take him on. I wouldn’t have any use for a man like that.”
sn3.24:1.16: a laborer youth would come along—untrained, unpracticed, undisciplined, undrilled, fearful, terrified, cowardly, quick to flee. Would you take him on? Would you have any use for a man like that?”
sn3.24:1.17: “No, lord, I wouldn’t take him on. I wouldn’t have any use for a man like that.”
sn3.24:2.1: “Now, what do you think, great king?
sn3.24:2.2: There is the case where you have a war at hand, a battle imminent.
sn3.24:2.3: A noble-warrior youth would come along—trained, practiced, disciplined, drilled, fearless, unterrified, not cowardly, not quick to flee.
sn3.24:2.4: Would you take him on? Would you have any use for a man like that?”
sn3.24:2.5: “Yes, lord, I would take him on. I would have use for a man like that.”
sn3.24:2.8: a laborer youth would come along—trained, practiced, disciplined, drilled, fearless, unterrified, not cowardly, not quick to flee.
sn3.24:2.9: Would take you him on? Would you have any use for a man like that?”
sn3.24:2.10: “Yes, lord, I would take him on. I would have use for a man like that.”
sn3.24:3.1: “In the same way, great king. When someone has gone forth from the home life into homelessness—no matter from what clan—and he has abandoned five factors and is endowed with five, what is given to him bears great fruit.
sn3.24:3.2: “And which five factors has he abandoned?
sn3.24:3.3: He has abandoned sensual desire… ill will… sloth & drowsiness… restlessness & anxiety… uncertainty.
sn3.24:3.4: These are the five factors he has abandoned.
sn3.24:3.5: And with which five factors is he endowed?
sn3.24:3.6: He is endowed with the aggregate of virtue of one beyond training… the aggregate of concentration of one beyond training… the aggregate of discernment of one beyond training… the aggregate of release of one beyond training… the aggregate of knowledge & vision of release of one beyond training.
sn3.24:3.7: These are the five factors with which he is endowed.
sn3.24:3.8: “What is given to one who has abandoned five factors and is endowed with five factors in this way bears great fruit.”
sn3.24:3.9: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn3.24:3.10: Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn3.24:4.1: “As a king intent on battle would hire a youth
sn3.24:4.2: in whom there are archery skills, persistence, & strength,
sn3.24:4.4: and not, on the basis of birth, a coward;
sn3.24:5.1: so, too, you should honor a person of noble conduct, wise,
sn3.24:5.2: in whom are established composure & patience,
sn3.24:5.4: even though his birth may be lowly.
sn3.24:6.2: and there invite the learned to stay.
sn3.24:6.3: Let them make reservoirs in dry forests
sn3.24:6.4: and walking paths where it’s rough.
sn3.24:7.1: Let them, with a clear, calm awareness, give food, drink, snacks,
sn3.24:8.1: Just as a hundred-billowed, lightning-garlanded, thundering cloud,
sn3.24:9.1: even so a person of conviction & learning, wise, having stored up provisions,
sn3.24:10.3: That is his thunder, like a raining cloud’s.
sn3.24:10.5: That shower of merit, abundant, rains back on the one who gives.”
